title: Synthesis vs simulation semantics
description: Simulation models timing and X-values; synthesis maps to gates and ignores delays/initial blocks, so the two can legitimately disagree.
body: Use when results differ between sim and hardware. Simulation honors #delays, initial blocks, and four-state (0/1/X/Z) logic; synthesis ignores delays/initial, infers hardware from supported constructs, and real gates have no X. Sources of mismatch: incomplete sensitivity lists, sim-only constructs, and X-pessimism. Do not trust a passing RTL sim as proof the gates work. Actions: use always_comb/always_ff, run gate-level sim or formal equivalence, and check reset X-handling. Validation signals include gate-level sim and equivalence-check pass. Risk boundary: deploying on RTL sim alone risks a silicon-only bug; require gate-level or formal sign-off.
